document: Crestholm Dynamics plc v Veltros Industries Inc — Skeleton Argument (KB-2025-002194)
note: The 12 citations from the skeleton's Citation Index. `expected_*` fields are our legal analysis to validate against the official rubric, NOT ground truth from the challenge.

citations.name: Lumley v Gye
citations.proposition: A defendant who knowingly and wilfully induces a third party to breach a subsisting contract is liable in damages.

citations.name: OBG Ltd v Allan
citations.proposition: Inducing breach requires actual knowledge of the contract, intention to procure breach, an actual breach, and resulting loss.

citations.name: DC Thomson & Co Ltd v Deakin
citations.proposition: For direct inducement the defendant need not know the precise terms, only that the contract exists.

citations.name: Fairfax International Logistics Ltd v Brennan Holdings plc
citations.proposition: A procurement conglomerate is treated as having intended the breach even where it had not read the contract.

citations.name: Hadley v Baxendale
citations.proposition: Two-limb remoteness test: loss arising naturally, or within reasonable contemplation of both parties.

citations.name: Transfield Shipping Inc v Mercator Shipping Inc (The Achilleas)
citations.proposition: Recoverability turns on whether the defendant assumed responsibility for the type of loss.

citations.name: Anglia Television Ltd v Reed
citations.proposition: Cited for: claimant may recover all expectation profits it would have earned had the contract been performed.
citations.expected_note: Anglia v Reed is a RELIANCE (wasted expenditure) case, not expectation/lost-profits.

citations.name: Stonegate Capital Partners Ltd v Redwood Procurement Group
citations.proposition: Court should adopt claimant's financial projections as the primary basis for lost-profit assessment.

citations.name: Wrotham Park Estate Co Ltd v Parkside Homes Ltd
citations.proposition: Cited for: damages by reference to the profits the claimant would have earned.
citations.expected_note: Real case but NOT in the 58-case corpus; and it is a NEGOTIATING-damages case, not lost profits.

citations.name: American Cyanamid Co v Ethicon Ltd
citations.proposition: Interim injunction: serious question to be tried, then balance of convenience.
citations.expected_note: In corpus as neutral citation [1975] UKHL 1 — must match by NAME, not citation form.

citations.name: Series 5 Software Ltd v Clarke
citations.proposition: Laddie J: applicant must show a strong prima facie case where the injunction would effectively decide the dispute.
citations.expected_note: Real case but NOT in the 58-case corpus → unverifiable, NOT fabricated.

citations.name: Pemberton Aerospace Systems Ltd v Delta Global Ventures Inc
citations.proposition: Displacement of a long-term exclusive aerospace supplier constitutes irreparable harm.
